message:
Infrastructure hardening: async session scan, symlink-cycle protection, I/O safety, and edge-case fixes

Seven independent improvements to core infrastructure modules:

scheduler.rs:
- Seq::next() and set_timeout use saturating_add to prevent overflow
- clear_timeout returns bool; only marks actually-pending timers as
  cancelled to avoid unbounded cancelled_timers growth
- New tests for saturation and double-cancel behavior

rpc.rs:
- RunningBash now carries a uuid::Uuid identity so the completion
  callback only clears the running-bash slot when its ID matches,
  fixing a race where a late completion could clear a newer run's entry

session.rs:
- scan_sessions_on_disk is now async: the blocking read_dir + metadata
  loop runs on a spawned thread with oneshot channel, avoiding blocking
  the async runtime during directory scans with many session files

session_picker.rs:
- Refactored delete_session_file to accept a configurable trash command
  via delete_session_file_with_trash_cmd for testability
- try_trash_with_cmd returns bool and logs warnings on failure, falling
  back to fs::remove_file instead of propagating trash errors
- Added tests for fallback-to-remove behavior

sse.rs:
- On hard UTF-8 decode errors, skip only the invalid byte sequence and
  retain the remainder of utf8_buffer, allowing the SSE stream to
  recover gracefully instead of losing all buffered data

tools.rs:
- Fix truncate_tail to trigger partial fallback when line_count == 1
  and byte_count == 0 (trailing empty line from files ending in \n)
- GrepTool and FindTool stderr channels now carry Result<Vec<u8>, String>
  so read errors propagate instead of being silently ignored
- Thread joins return errors instead of silently swallowing panics
- FindTool refactored to join handles directly instead of via channels

resources.rs:
- load_skills_from_dir gains symlink-cycle protection: tracks visited
  canonical paths in a HashSet, skipping directories already visited
  to prevent infinite recursion through circular symlinks
- Unix-only test for symlink cycle behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
